title: Hieracium eriocerinthe
taxonomy: Hieracium eriocerinthe is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. Within the Asteraceae family, it belongs to the tribe Cichorieae. The genus Hieracium is a group of hawkweeds, which are typically classified as either true hawkweeds (subgenus Pilosella) or false hawkweeds (subgenus Hieracium). Hieracium eriocerinthe is specifically placed within the subgenus Hieracium. This classification helps to distinguish Hieracium eriocerinthe from other related plant species.

intro: Hieracium eriocerinthe, commonly known as hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium eriocerinthe include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with hawkweed include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
